name: On the shores of the Entrepeñas Reservoir — circular from Mantiel along the Tajo Natural Path
name_original: A orillas del Embalse de Entrepeñas — circular desde Mantiel por el camino Natural del Tajo
region_intro: Hiking around Mantiel offers access to the distinctive "Alcarreño" landscape, characterized by olive groves, abandoned orchards, and mixed forests of pines, junipers, and holm oaks. The region's topography, hydrology, and geology contribute to a varied environmental setting. A prominent natural feature is the proximity to the Entrepeñas reservoir, providing scenic vistas along many routes. This area presents diverse recreational possibilities for outdoor enthusiasts. Best hiking trails around Mantiel The most popular hiking route is El Garduño de Cela Trail, a 7.2 miles (11.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 25 minutes to complete. This moderate route traverses through the characteristic Alcarreño landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Tetas de Viana Loop, a difficult 4.2 miles (6.8 km) path. This route offers views from the Tetas de Viana hills. Local hikers also love the On the shores of the Entrepeñas Reservoir — circular from Mantiel along the Tajo Natural Path, a 13.4 miles (21.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain along the reservoir, often completed in about 6 hours 15 minutes. Hiking around Mantiel is defined by scenic reservoir views, mixed forests, and the characteristic Alcarreño landscape. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Mantiel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 150 hikers have used komoot to explore Mantiel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find around Mantiel?
A: Mantiel offers a diverse range of hiking trails, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The landscape is characterized by its "Alcarreño" features, including olive groves, mixed forests of pines, junipers, and holm oaks, and scenic views of the Entrepeñas reservoir. There are 7 recorded tours in the area, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, Mantiel has easy hiking options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38150643" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dique de Pareja – Azud de Pareja loop from Pareja&lt;/a&gt; is an easy route, perfect for a relaxed stroll. Overall, there are 2 easy tours available in the region.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Mantiel?
A: Absolutely. Many trails around Mantiel are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A notable example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030109677/tetas-de-viana-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tetas de Viana Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers marvelous views from the Tetas de Viana hills.

Q: What are some natural attractions or landmarks to see while hiking in Mantiel?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can enjoy stunning views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5369072" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrepeñas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; along many routes. Other highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6689105" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tetas de Viana (1,145 m)&lt;/a&gt; summits and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5902743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cifuentes River Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The area's characteristic Alcarreño landscape with its olive groves and mixed forests is a constant scenic backdrop.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Mantiel?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' routes aren't explicitly categorized, the easy trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38150643" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dique de Pareja – Azud de Pareja loop from Pareja&lt;/a&gt;, are generally suitable for families looking for a less strenuous outdoor experience. The varied terrain and scenic reservoir views offer engaging walks for all ages.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Mantiel?
A: The trails around Mantiel are generally accessible throughout the year. However, if hiking during summer, it's advisable to start early in the day and carry ample water due to higher temperatures. Spring and autumn offer pleasant weather for exploring the Alcarreño landscape and reservoir views.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Mantiel?
A: The hiking routes in Mantiel are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the scenic reservoir views, the diverse mixed forests, and the unique Alcarreño landscape that defines the region's trails.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking paths that pass through Mantiel?
A: Yes, Mantiel is located on the &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mantiel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR113 (Sendero de Gran Recorrido)&lt;/a&gt;, also known as the Natural Way of the Tagus. This long-distance path stretches from Albarracín to Lisbon, offering opportunities for extended treks through the region.

Q: What is the typical duration for hikes around Mantiel?
A: Hikes around Mantiel vary significantly in duration depending on the route and your pace. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030489046/el-garduno-de-cela-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Garduño de Cela Trail&lt;/a&gt; typically takes about 3 hours and 25 minutes, while the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022641343/on-the-shores-of-the-entrepenas-reservoir-circular-from-mantiel-along-the-tajo-natural-path" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;On the shores of the Entrepeñas Reservoir — circular from Mantiel along the Tajo Natural Path&lt;/a&gt; can take around 6 hours and 15 minutes.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or panoramic spots worth visiting?
A: Yes, the region offers several excellent viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430862" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Mantiel and the Entrepeñas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a popular spot, as is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5906179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Alocén&lt;/a&gt;. Views from the Tetas de Viana hills, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17506987" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tetas de Viana (1,145 m) loop from Viana de Mondéjar&lt;/a&gt;, are also highly recommended for their expansive panoramas.

Q: Is Mantiel a good place for wildlife spotting during hikes?
A: The mixed forests of pines, junipers, and holm oaks, along with the proximity to the Entrepeñas reservoir, create a diverse habitat. While specific wildlife sightings are not guaranteed, the natural environment suggests opportunities for observing local flora and fauna characteristic of the Alcarreño landscape.
tips:
This small town is located near the bed of one of the most important tributaries of the Tagus River, at the gate to the Sierra de Umbría Negra.

It was once famous for its baths, of which today only the old building remains, but it still preserves the flavor and freshness of the nature that surrounds it, and some landscapes to recreate the view, as well as some beautiful fountains from the s. XVIII.

The Entrepeñas reservoir extends for several kilometers over the course of the Tagus River, one of the largest in Spain. In this sector of Guadalajara you can explore it following the track that runs parallel to the riverbed through the gorges until you reach the town of Trillo.

waypoints.name: View of Mantiel and the Entrepeñas Reservoir
